channel.title: 2026-05-07 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-05-07 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: Deep Wave Network for Modeling Multi-Scale Physical Dynamics
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Alexander I. Khrabry, Edward A. Startsev, Andrew T. Powis, Igor D. Kaganovich
arXiv:2605.04198v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Performance of deep learning models is strongly governed by architectural capacity, with width and depth as primary controls. However, in physical-science applications, models are often compared at a single fixed size or by separating accuracy and computational cost, which can be misleading since architectures exhibit different accuracy-cost scaling as width and depth vary. This issue is particularly relevant for U-Net-type encoder-decoder models, widely used for multi-scale gas, fluid, and plasma dynamics due to their ability to represent features across spatial scales. A U-Net constructs a multi-resolution representation via an encoder that progressively reduces spatial resolution, followed by a decoder that restores it for prediction. S…

item.title: Multistate Coupled Diabatic Neural Network potential for the quantum non-adiabatic Photofragmentation of CH₂⁺
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Pablo del Mazo-Sevillano, Susana Gomez-Carrasco, Alfredo Aguado, Octavio Roncero
arXiv:2605.04756v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tracking the complex non-adiabatic transitions in far-ultraviolet photodissociation demands highly accurate diabatic potential energy matrices (PEMs) across numerous excited states. To address this, we introduce a fully automated diabatization method that leverages artificial neural networks to fit PEMs. Our approach divides the PEM into a physically grounded zeroth-order diagonal term, which is then corrected by a neural network matrix to capture electronic couplings. By enforcing symmetry constraints on off-diagonal elements and sharing degenerate diabatic states between the A' and A'' irreducible representations, the diabatization process becomes completely automatic. We validate this method using time-dependent wavepacket calculations to…

item.title: A physics-informed neural network approach to solve the spatially inhomogeneous electron Boltzmann equation
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Ihda Chaerony Siffa, Detlef Loffhagen, Markus M. Becker, Jan Trieschmann
arXiv:2605.04307v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The accurate determination of electron properties is fundamental to low-temperature plasma simulations, necessitating precise solutions to the spatially inhomogeneous electron Boltzmann equation (EBE). This work explores the use of physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) for obtaining solutions to the spatially one-dimensional (1D) EBE subject to a uniform electric field in atomic gases. Employing the two-term approximation, the resulting equation for the isotropic distribution is solved directly in kinetic energy space without the conventional transformation to total energy. This approach demonstrates the flexibility of the PINN framework in handling diverse equation formulations. To address the convergence difficulties associated with thi…

item.title: Beyond Rigid Geometries: The Spline-Pullback Metric for Universal Diffeomorphic SPD Representation Learning
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Tushar Das, Subrata Dutta, Sarmistha Neogy, Koushlendra Kumar Singh
arXiv:2605.04406v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The integration of Symmetric Positive Definite (SPD) matrices into deep learning has historically relied on fixed algebraic Riemannian metrics. Analogous to hand-crafted features in classical machine learning, these static formulations impose rigid geometries limiting network expressivity and adaptability. Recent attempts to parameterize these geometries often violate the axioms of primary matrix functions through unconstrained powers or rank-dependent scaling, inviting spatial folding, loss of global surjectivity, and gradient collapse at spectral singularities. In this paper, we introduce the Spline-Pullback Metric (SPM), instantiated as Spectral-SPM and Cholesky-SPM, marking a paradigm shift from static metric selection to universal geome…

item.title: Cross-Model Consistency of Feature Importance in Electrospinning: Separating Robust from Model-Dependent Features
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Mehrab Mahdian, Ferenc Ender, Tamas Pardy
arXiv:2605.04905v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Electrospinning is a highly sensitive fabrication process in which small variations in operating parameters can significantly influence fiber morphology and material performance. Machine learning (ML) methods are increasingly employed to model these process-structure relationships and to identify the relative importance of processing variables. However, most existing studies rely on a single ML model, implicitly assuming that the resulting feature importance is robust and reproducible. In this study, the consistency of feature importance across multiple ML model families was systematically evaluated using a curated dataset of 96 polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) electrospinning experiments. Twenty-one ML models representing linear, tree-based, kernel-…

item.title: Neural network modeling of many-body super- and sub-radiant dynamics
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Gianluca Lagnese, Laurin Brunner, Lorenzo Rossi, Darrick Chang, Markus Schmitt, Zala Lenarčič
arXiv:2605.04640v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: There is significant interest in exploring novel phenomena in quantum light-matter interfaces, which are driven by the combination of structured dissipation and long-range interactions that are typical in such systems. To this end, it is important to develop new general numerical simulation techniques, which can access large system sizes and are not based on semi-classical approaches. Here, we report the first application of neural quantum states to obtain the dissipative dynamics of light-matter-coupled systems beyond what is accessible with exact and tensor-network calculations. We specifically apply this method to simulate the many-body emission dynamics of approximately 40 atoms, arranged in dense arrays in one and two dimensions. Thes…

item.title: Spin Dynamics from Atomistic Quantum Simulations
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Enrico Drigo, Marquis M. McMillan, Benjamin Pingault, Yinan Dong, F. Joseph Heremans, David D. Awschalom, Giulia Galli
arXiv:2605.04294v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Optically active solid-state spin defects are promising candidates for quantum applications, however a unified theoretical framework to predict their spin dynamics at high temperatures is not yet available. Here, using Kubo linear--response theory, we derive expressions of spin-lattice and decoherence times (T₁) and (T₂) in terms of correlation functions of spin--lattice couplings. We then evaluate (T₁) and (T₂) from molecular dynamics and spin--lattice interaction time--series generated by state--of--the--art machine learning models trained on it ab--initio data. Finally we measure (T₁) times for the NV center in diamond and compare experimental and theoretical results, showing excellent agreement.

item.title: A global spacetime optimization approach to the real-space time-dependent Schrödinger equation
item.description:
📚 Machine Learning: Science and Technology
👤 Enze Hou, Yuzhi Liu, Linxuan Zhang, Difa Ye, Lei Wang and Han Wang
The time-dependent Schrödinger equation (TDSE) in real space is fundamental to understanding the dynamics of many-electron quantum systems, with applications ranging from quantum chemistry to condensed matter physics and materials science. However, solving the TDSE for complex fermionic systems remains a significant challenge, particularly due to the need to capture the time-evolving many-body correlations, while the antisymmetric nature of fermionic wavefunctions complicates the function space in which these solutions must be represented. We propose a general-purpose neural network framework for solving the real-space TDSE, Fermionic Antisymmetric Spatiotemporal Network, which treats time as an explicit input alongside spatial coordinates, enabling a unified spatiotemporal representation…

item.title: Assessing Claims of Advantage in Quantum Machine Learning: A Critical Review
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Stefan Klug, Maximilian Moll
Quantum machine learning (QML) has attracted growing interest because quantum states and circuits may provide new representational primitives for learning, yet progress remains constrained by noisy intermediate-scale quantum (NISQ) hardware and heterogeneous evaluation practices. This article presents a critical review of recent QML architectures and representative empirical and theoretical studies, with emphasis on work published since 2022 alongside selected seminal references. To improve comparability, “quantum advantage” is assessed in three distinct senses: computational scaling under stated input-access assumptions, empirical predictive performance in the benchmark setting considered, and resource efficiency at a fixed budget accounting for both classical compute and quantum executi…

item.title: 深度学习赋能的可编程拓扑电路
item.description:
📚 Nature Communications
👤 Hao Jia, Shanglin Yang, Jiajun He, Shuo Liu, Haoxiang Chen, Ce Shang 等12位作者
该研究开发了基于深度学习的可编程拓扑电路平台，实现对晶格哈密顿量的灵活调控。作者在高阶拓扑体系中观测到边界态、绝热相变和平带朗道能级，并实现了位置可控的安德森局域化，展示了在信息加密与防伪领域的应用潜力。

item.title: Scalable quantum dynamics compilation via quantum machine learning
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Yuxuan Zhang, Roeland Wiersema, Juan Carrasquilla, Lukasz Cincio, and Yong Baek Kim
Author(s): Yuxuan Zhang, Roeland Wiersema, Juan Carrasquilla, Lukasz Cincio, and Yong Baek Kim Quantum dynamics compilation is an important task for improving quantum simulation efficiency: It aims to synthesize multiqubit target dynamics into a circuit consisting of as few elementary gates as possible. Compared to deterministic methods such as Trotterization, variational quantum compilation … [Phys. Rev. Research 8, 023128] Published Thu May 07, 2026

item.title: Full-chip CMP modelling based on Fully Convolutional Network leveraging White Light Interferometry
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jules Exbrayat, Renan Bouis, Elie Sezestre, Viorel Balan, Arnaud Cornelis, Damien Hebras, Catherine Euvrard
arXiv:2605.05062v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As time-to-market is crucial in the Integrated Circuit (IC) industry, speeding up layout manufacturability verifi-cation is essential. Chemical-Mechanical Polishing (CMP) plays a vital role in IC fabrication but is significantly influenced by Layout-Dependent Effects (LDE). An accurate and efficient CMP model enables design teams to correct surface unevenness before fabrication, reducing costs and accelerating the design phase. However, existing models often rely on Density Step Height (DSH) modeling, which is time-consuming for calibration and requires substantial hardware resources for fine-grained predictions. In this paper, we propose combining the advantages of two surface analysis techniques, White Light Interfer-ometry (WLI) and Atomi…

item.title: Machine learning inference of fission yields from gamma spectroscopy for very low-yield nuclear test verification
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Julien de Troullioud de Lanversin, Jiehui Li, Christopher Fichtlscherer, Dongdong She, Moritz Kutt
arXiv:2605.04674v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Very low-yield nuclear tests pose a major verification challenge for the zero-yield standard of the Comprehensive Nuclear-Test-Ban Treaty (CTBT). The zero-yield standard prohibits any explosive experiment that produces a self-sustaining fission chain reaction while allowing subcritical experiments. Previous research shows that on-site gamma spectroscopy of post-test debris provides useful insight into the criticality level, although it remains heavily dependent on knowledge of certain experimental settings. Here, we adopt a new approach whereby machine learning models are trained on simulated gamma spectroscopy data to infer the fission yield of a nuclear very low-yield test. Using high-fidelity 3D Monte Carlo particle transport simulations,…

item.title: Meta-LegNet: A Transferable and Interpretable Framework for Surface Adsorption Prediction via Self-Defined Adsorption-Environment Learning
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yifan Li, Arravind Subramanian, Xiaoqing Liu, Qiujie Lyu, Sergey Kozlov, Lei Shen
arXiv:2605.04102v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A central challenge in computational catalysis is the identification of low-energy and chemically plausible adsorption configurations, as these directly affect adsorption energies, reaction pathways, and catalytic performance. Existing approaches generally rely on enumerating candidate adsorption sites followed by iterative refinement through density functional theory calculations or machine-learning-based relaxations. However, such workflows remain computationally expensive and are difficult to scale to complex surfaces or multi-adsorbate systems. Here, we introduce Meta-LegNet, a graph learning framework that combines SE(3)-equivariant atom-level message passing with voxel-based multiscale aggregation and cross-domain meta-learning to lear…

item.title: Accelerating Sustainable Glass Discovery: Integrating Molecular Dynamics, Machine Learning, and Robotic Synthesis
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Felix Arendt, Tina Waurischk, Stefan Reinsch, Andrea S. S. Camargo, Marek Sierka
Discovering sustainable glass compositions demands navigating vast chemical spaces – a challenge that conventional experimentation cannot meet efficiently. Here we introduce the Simulation-Calibrated Active Learning Estimator (SCALE), a closed-loop framework uniting high-throughput molecular dynamics (MD), machine learning (ML), and robotic synthesis to bridge the gap between simulation and experiment. Over 42,000 melt–quench MD simulations with compact cells (≈ 200–500 atoms) train ML models that predict density and elastic moduli with cross-validated R2 values up to 0.98. Comparison with 55 robotically synthesized sodium alumino-borosilicate glasses reveals systematic density overestimation of up to 5%. Rather than naively augmenting training data, SCALE iteratively learns a composition…

item.title: Enhancing dimensionality prediction in hybrid metal halides via feature engineering and class-imbalance mitigation
item.description:
📚 Machine Learning: Science and Technology
👤 Mariia Karabin, Isaac Armstrong, Leo Beck, Paulina Apanel, Markus Eisenbach, David B Mitzi, Hanna Terletska and Hendrik Heinz
We present a machine learning (ML) framework for predicting the structural dimensionality of hybrid metal halides (HMHs), including organic-inorganic perovskites, using a combination of chemically-informed feature engineering and advanced class-imbalance handling techniques. This study is motivated by the small and highly imbalanced nature of experimentally available HMH datasets, which limits the applicability and reliability of conventional ML approaches. The dataset, consisting of 494 HMH structures, is highly imbalanced across dimensionality classes (0D, 1D, 2D, 3D), posing significant challenges to predictive modeling. To mitigate this limitation, the dataset was augmented to 1336 samples using the synthetic minority oversampling technique, enabling improved learning of underrepresen…

item.title: A Mean Curvature Approach to Boundary Detection: Geometric Insights for Unsupervised Learning
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Alexandre L. M. Levada
arXiv:2605.04274v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accurate boundary detection in high-dimensional data remains a central challenge in unsupervised learning, particularly in the presence of non-linear structures and heterogeneous densities. In this work, we introduce Mean Curvature Boundary Points (MCBP), a novel geometric framework grounded in Geometric Machine Learning that departs from traditional density-based approaches by explicitly modeling the intrinsic curvature of the data manifold. The method relies on a discrete approximation of the shape operator, estimated from local k-nearest neighbor patches, to compute pointwise mean curvature without requiring explicit manifold parametrization. The key insight of MCBP is to use mean curvature as a principled descriptor of boundary structure…

item.title: Physics-informed machine learning for spectrum prediction in phosphorescent OLEDs
item.description:
📚 npj Computational Materials
👤 Hasup Lee, Hyuntae Cho, Hwidong Na, Kuhwan Jeong, Sang Ha Park, Kisoo Kwon 等12位作者
npj Computational Materials, Published online: 07 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41524-026-02078-x Physics-informed machine learning for spectrum prediction in phosphorescent OLEDs

item.title: 面向力学正逆分析的鲁棒约束权重物理信息神经网络
item.description:
📚 ScienceDirect
该工作提出一种鲁棒的约束权重物理信息神经网络（PINN），用于力学问题的正向与逆向分析。通过约束网络权重机制增强物理约束的嵌入，提升在固体力学、流体力学等场景中求解偏微分方程及反问题时的稳定性与精度，详情需查阅原文获取具体方法细节与验证结果。

item.title: Platonic representation of foundation machine learning interatomic potentials
item.description:
📚 Nature Machine Intelligence
👤 Zhenzhu Li, Aron Walsh
Nature Machine Intelligence, Published online: 07 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s42256-026-01235-7 Li and Walsh show that a unified ‘platonic’ geometry emerges across the representations of independent foundation models for learning interatomic potentials. The observation enables cross-model comparison, embedding arithmetic and ground-truth-free diagnostics.

item.title: Boundary Renormalization Group Flow of Entanglement Entropy at a (2+1)-Dimensional Quantum Critical Point
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Zhiyan Wang, Zhe Wang, Yi-Ming Ding, Zenan Liu, Zheng Yan, Long Zhang
arXiv:2509.02044v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We investigate the second-order Rényi entanglement entropy at the quantum critical point of a spin-1/2 antiferromagnetic Heisenberg model on a columnar dimerized square lattice. The universal constant γ in the area-law scaling S₂(ell) = αell - γ is found to be sensitive to the entangling surface configurations, with γₛₚ > 0 for strong-bond-cut (special) surfaces and γₒᵣd < 0 for weak-bond-cut (ordinary) surfaces, which is attributed to the distinct conformal boundary conditions. Introducing boundary dimerization drives a renormalization group (RG) flow from the special to the ordinary boundary criticality, and the constant γ decreases monotonically with increasing dimerization strength, demonstrating irreversible evolution under the boun…

item.title: Dual-Switch Control of a Layer-Locked Anomalous Valley Hall Effect in a Sliding Ferroelectric Antiferromagnet
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Quan Shen, Wenhu Liao, Degao Xu, Jiansheng Dong, Jianing Tan
arXiv:2601.19556v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: The integration of ferroelectric (FE) and antiferromagnetic (AFM) orders in twodimensional (2D) materials provides a promising avenue for the nonvolatile control of coupled spin and valley degrees of freedom, a capability central to advancing spinvalleytronics. However, realizing a single material system where these quantum states can be independently and reversibly manipulated by distinct stimuli, a prerequisite for multifunctional devices, has remained elusive. Here, we demonstrate a dual-switch mechanism in bilayer VS2, a room-temperature FE-AFM system, that enables electrical and magnetic control of a layer-locked anomalous valley Hall effect (AVHE). First-principles calculations reveal that interlayer sliding breaks spatial inversio…

item.title: Fate of Topological Dirac Magnons in van der Waals Ferromagnets at Finite Temperature
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Rintaro Eto, Ignacio Salgado-Linares, Masahito Mochizuki, Johannes Knolle, Alexander Mook
arXiv:2509.13900v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Dirac magnons, the bosonic counterparts of Dirac fermions in graphene, provide a unique platform to explore symmetry-protected band crossings and quantum geometry in magnetic insulators, while promising high-velocity, low-dissipation spin transport for next-generation magnonic technologies. However, their stability under realistic, finite-temperature conditions remains an open question. Here, we develop a comprehensive microscopic theory of thermal magnon-magnon interactions in van der Waals honeycomb ferromagnets, focusing on both gapless and gapped Dirac magnons. Using nonlinear spin-wave theory with magnon self-energy corrections and a T-matrix resummation that captures two-magnon bound states, we quantitatively reproduce temperature-…

item.title: Free fermionic and parafermionic multispin quantum chains with non-homogeneous interacting ranges
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Francisco C. Alcaraz
arXiv:2512.08011v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: A large family of multispin interacting one-dimensional quantum spin models with Z(N) symmetry and a free-particle eigenspectra are known in the literature. They are free-fermionic (N=2) and free-parafermionic (N≥ 2) quantum chains. The essential ingredient that implies the free-particle spectra is the fact that these Hamiltonians are expressed in terms of generators of a Z(N) exchange algebra. In all these known quantum chains the number of spins in all the multispin interactions (range of interactions) is the same and therefore, the models have homogeneous interacting range. In this paper we extend the Z(N) exchange algebra, by introducing new models with a free-particle spectra, where the interaction ranges of the multispin interactio…

item.title: Superconductivity from Quasiparticle Pairing of Intervalley Coherent State in Rhombohedral Trilayer Graphene
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Chun Wang Chau, Shuai A. Chen, K. T. Law
arXiv:2404.19237v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Superconductivity is observed in rhombohedral trilayer graphene in a narrow regime between the flavor-symmetric state and the symmetry breaking phase, which cannot be described by the conventional Bardeen-Cooper-Schrieffer theory. The measured coherence length, for instance, is roughly two orders of magnitude shorter than the value predicted by the Bardeen-Cooper-Schrieffer relation based on the large fermi velocity and an extremely low charge carrier density of the flavor-symmetric phase. To resolve the discrepancies, we propose that the rhombohedral trilayer graphene superconducting phase arises from the pairing of quasiparticles of the adjacent inter-valley coherent state. We illustrate the superconducting phenomenology using gapped D…

item.title: Topological Classification of Dynamical Quantum Phase Transitions in the 1D XY model via Critical Mode Analysis
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Bao-Ming Xu
arXiv:2408.00597v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Dynamical quantum phase transitions (DQPTs), which serve as a theoretical framework for understanding far-from-equilibrium physics in quantum many-body systems, have recently been observed experimentally. Their topological properties are typically characterized by the winding number, which acts as an order parameter. While DQPTs exhibiting both integer and half-integer jumps in the winding number have been reported, the underlying mechanisms behind these distinct topological behaviors, as well as the potential existence of other topological classes, remain open questions. To address this, we investigate DQPTs in the one-dimensional XY model under a quench protocol. We show that the observed topological diversity originates from the natur…

item.title: Towards reliable electrical measurements of superconducting devices inside a transmission electron microscope
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Joachim Dahl Thomsen, Michael I. Faley, Joseph Vimal Vas, Alexander Clausen, Thibaud Denneulin, Dominik Biscette, Denys Sutter, Peng-Han Lu, Rafal E. Dunin-Borkowski
arXiv:2601.13298v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Correlating structure with electronic functionality is central to the engineering of quantum materials and devices whose properties depend sensitively on disorder. Transmission electron microscopy (TEM) offers high spatial resolution together with access to structural, electronic, and magnetic degrees of freedom. However, operando electrical transport measurements on functional quantum devices remain rare, particularly at liquid helium temperature. Here, we demonstrate electrical transport measurements of niobium nitride (NbN) devices inside a TEM using a continuous-flow liquid-helium-cooled sample holder. By optimizing a thermal radiation shield to limit radiation from the nearby pole pieces of the objective lens, we achieve an estimate…

item.title: Two-Qubit Module Based on Phonon-Coupled Ge Hole-Spin Qubits: Design, Fabrication, and Readout at 1-4 K
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 D. -M. Mei, S. A. Panamaldeniya, K. -M. Dong, S. Bhattarai, A. Prem
arXiv:2601.01704v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We present a device-level design study for a two-qubit module based on phonon-coupled germanium (Ge) hole-spin qubits targeted for operation at 1--4~K. Building on prior theoretical modeling of phonon-engineered Ge qubits and phononic-crystal (PnC) cavities, we translate those modeling results into a fabrication-oriented two-qubit layout that integrates two gate-defined hole-spin qubits in a strained Ge quantum well with a GHz PnC defect mode intended to mediate a coherent phonon-based interaction. We specify the SiGe/Ge heterostructure, electrostatic gate layout, PnC cavity geometry, and a compatible nanofabrication pathway, including gate-stack formation, membrane patterning and release, RF/DC wiring, and process-risk mitigation. We fu…

item.title: All-electrical switching of spin texture in a strain-tunable 2D Janus ferroelectric altermagnet
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Tao Yao, Quan Shen, Wenhu Liao, Jianing Tan, Jiansheng Dong
arXiv:2601.19560v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Altermagnetism (AM), a collinear magnetic phase with momentum-dependent spin splitting, is a promising candidate for strong magnetoelectric coupling. However, realizing direct and tunable coupling between ferroelectricity (FE) and AM within a single two-dimensional (2D) material remains an outstanding challenge. Here, based on first-principles calculations, we identify the distorted phase of monolayer Janus VOClBr as an intrinsic 2D FE-AM. This phase demonstrates robust magnetoelectric coupling, as evidenced by a complete reversal of momentum-space spin polarization upon FE switching, and further supported by spin texture analysis and the magneto-optical Kerr effect. Notably, the FE properties are highly strain-tunable: biaxial compressi…

item.title: Continuum limit of gauged tensor network states
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Gertian Roose, Erez Zohar
arXiv:2511.10189v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: It is well known that all physically relevant states of gauge theories lie in the sectors of the Hilbert space which satisfy the Gauss law. On the lattice, the manifeslty gauge invariant subspace is known to be exactly spanned by gauged tensor networks. In this work, we demonstrate that the continuum limit of certain types of gauged tensor networks is well defined and leads to a new class of states that may be helpful for the non-perturbative study of gauge theories directly in the continuum.

item.title: Mn和Ti掺杂调控BaFe₁₂O₁₉六方铁氧体的介电、磁性与磁介电行为
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Xiao-Fan Zhang, Yang Yang, Ze-Qing Guo, Li Lv, Can Gao, Jian-Ping Zhou, Xiao-ming Chen
该工作制备了Mn、Ti单掺及共掺BaFe₁₂O₁₉六方铁氧体，通过Raman光谱和形成能分析确定Mn优先占据4f₂和2b位，Ti主要替代4f₁和12k位的Fe³⁺。Ti⁴⁺掺杂通过调节超交换相互作用和降低c轴单轴磁晶各向异性，稳定了非共线纵向圆锥自旋序；纯相呈现低温量子顺电行为，Mn-Ti共掺杂因三角双锥内电偶极子解耦而破坏该行为，电子跃迁与极化子效应主导介电响应。

item.title: 耗散XXZ自旋链中的动力学关联
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Cătălin Paşcu Moca, Doru Sticlet, Ovidiu I. Pâţu, Balazs Dóra
该工作研究具有均匀局域自旋损失与泵浦的耗散XXZ自旋链中的动力学自旋关联。从无特征但具有有限磁化的混合稳态出发，发现纵向关联子在幺正演化下重现了自旋输运普适类层次结构（弹道、KPZ超扩散、扩散），而有限磁化会叠加额外的弹道光锥传播；横向关联子在易面情形呈指数快速衰减，各向同性强耦合下遵循KPZ标度，易轴情形则为弹道展宽。在完整Lindblad动力学下，早期时间普适类得以保持，关联整体呈指数衰减。

item.title: 原子重构与各向异性屏蔽影响下moiré激子的有效长程吸引
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Nils-Erik Schütte, Carl Emil Mørch Nielsen, Niclas Götting, Alexander Steinhoff, Gabriel Bester, Christopher Gies
该工作针对过渡金属硫族化合物双层moiré超晶格中的层间激子，建立了包含晶格重构和各向异性介电屏蔽的材料真实相互作用模型。发现晶格重构显著改变在位相互作用，而moiré周期尺度上的长程相互作用因各向异性屏蔽出现从排斥到吸引的 crossover；所得相互作用势与跃迁振幅作为Bose-Hubbard模型参数，成功解释了层间激子的关联行为，为理解moiré激子多体物理提供了微观基础。

item.title: From Classical to Quantum-Mechanical Data Assimilation: A Comparison between DATO and QMDA
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Emanuele Donno, Giovanni Conti, Paolo Oddo, Silvio Gualdi, Luca Mainetti, Giovanni Aloisio
arXiv:2605.04881v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Data assimilation provides a systematic framework for combining dynamical models with partial and noisy observations to infer the evolving state of a system. In this work, we undertake a comparative study of Data Assimilation with Transfer Operators (DATO) and Quantum Mechanical Data Assimilation (QMDA), focusing on their mathematical formulation, algorithmic structure, and empirical performance. Both methods are first cast within a common operator-theoretic framework, which makes it possible to compare, on a unified basis, their representations of uncertainty, forecast propagation, and assimilation updates. We then analyse their principal similarities and differences with respect to state-space structure, update mechanisms, structural pre…

item.title: 混合Kitaev自旋-轨道模型中的阻挫磁序
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Ivan Dutta, Aayush Vijayvargia, Anamitra Mukherjee, Onur Erten, Kush Saha
该工作研究将两种原本定义于不同晶格几何的可精确求解自旋液体哈密顿量（Kitaev蜂窝格点模型与Yao-Lee模型）组合到共同晶格环境时基态的演化。通过自洽平均场分析与微扰计算，发现强Kitaev区域自旋 sector 出现磁序而轨道 sector 保持拓扑序；Yao-Lee与方格模型杂化展现出丰富的Majorana狄拉克能带演化与Lifshitz转变，且在特定耦合参数下恢复单一流动的Majorana费米子的精确可解性。

item.title: 量子临界点非线性淬灭中的扭结-扭结关联
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Lakshita Jindal, Kavita Jain
该工作研究一维横场伊辛模型中代数变化淬越过量子临界点后的等时纵向扭结-扭结关联函数。超越Kibble-Zurek机制，探讨有限相位差导致的附加长度尺度及关联函数非指数衰减等非普适特征的适用范围。针对非线性淬灭，分析扭结对关联在参数空间中的行为，揭示淬灭动力学中除KZ标度外的额外标度结构，为理解量子临界动力学中的高阶关联提供新视角。

item.title: Learning Time-Varying Graphs from Incomplete Graph Signals
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Chuansen Peng, Xiaojing Shen
arXiv:2510.17903v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: This paper tackles the challenging problem of jointly inferring time-varying network topologies and imputing missing data from partially observed graph signals. We propose a unified non-convex optimization framework to simultaneously recover a sequence of graph Laplacian matrices while reconstructing the unobserved signal entries. Unlike conventional decoupled methods, our integrated approach facilitates a bidirectional flow of information between the graph and signal domains, yielding superior robustness, particularly in high missing-data regimes. To capture realistic network dynamics, we introduce a fused-lasso type regularizer on the sequence of Laplacians. This penalty promotes temporal smoothness by penalizing large successive chang…

item.title: 自旋极化螺旋边缘态的磁致发光与纳米尺度成像
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Samuel Haeuser, Richard H. J. Kim, Lin-Lin Wang, Thomas Koschny, Pedro M. Lozano, Genda Gu, Randall K. Chan, Joong-Mok Park, Martin Mootz, Liang Luo, Qiang Li, Jigang Wang
该工作利用低温磁红外散射型扫描近场光学显微镜(cm-IR-sSNOM)，实现红外螺旋边缘态的磁致发光与纳米尺度可视化。测量揭示磁场诱导的台阶边缘近场电导率，发现量子自旋霍尔自旋劈裂模具有增强的红外极化率与略窄化的近场轮廓。红外边缘电动力学响应随原子层数近似线性标度，表明磁场诱导的能隙在约100 meV能量处不破坏单层边缘态，为拓扑保护边缘模式的纳米尺度输运表征提供新途径。

item.title: 磁场对浓固溶体中离子输运影响的解析研究
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Timothy Carlson, Sanjay Govindjee
该工作解析研究了磁场对固体离子导体中离子输运的影响，建立了考虑磁场效应的多组分输运方程，并针对各向同性二元导体和单离子导体推导了具体模型。研究计算了特定体系中磁场效应可能显著的材料参数组合，并验证所得二元导体模型与实验磁阻数据吻合良好，为理解磁场调控固态电化学过程提供了理论基础。

item.title: 未掺杂一维Anderson与Kondo晶格中库仑增强超导性到近长程磁序的映射
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 J. E. Ebot, Lorenzo Pizzino, Sam Mardazad, Johannes S. Hofmann, Thierry Giamarchi, Adrian Kantian
该工作将Anderson晶格模型精确映射为连接金属库的配对超导层模型，首次大规模准精确数值研究一维Anderson晶格及其微扰导出的一维Kondo项链模型。发现在绝缘态建立的特征长度尺度以下，超导关联与密度-密度关联简并且均趋于近有序态，其程度远超任何孤立短程相互作用一维配对层。该效应源于金属层介导的有效长程耦合，揭示了库仑增强超导与周期Kondo系统的深层联系。

item.title: 量子磁体中的冷却致熔化
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 K. Jaksetič, T. Arh, M. Pregelj, M. Gomilšek, M. Dragomir, P. Prelovšek, M. Ulaga, L. Šibav, M. Malovrh, K. Železnikar, Z. Jagličić, P. Manuel, F. Orlandi, D. Khalyavin, M. D. Le, N. Bujault, E. Lhotel, J. van Tol, U. Jena, B. Sana, P. Khuntia, A. Zorko
该工作在被阻挫的Ising型三角晶格反铁磁体七钽酸铒中发现了反常的"自旋Pomeranchuk效应"：降温过程中体系先形成三子格长程磁有序（类固体），随后在更低温度下意外熔化为短程关联的自旋条纹态（类液体）。研究提出这种逆熔化现象可普遍源于强自旋-自旋相互作用竞争，为奇异磁相之间的转变提供了新机制，将³He的Pomeranchuk效应范式拓展至量子磁性领域。

item.title: 共线自旋-轨道磁体中的混合宇称交错磁性
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Zheng-Yang Zhuang, Jin-Xin Hu, Song-Bo Zhang, Lun-Hui Hu, Zhongbo Yan
该工作证明零净磁化的共线反铁磁体可承载既非纯偶也非纯奇动量依赖性的混合宇称自旋劈裂。二维自旋-轨道磁体中，当两个反平行自旋 sector 由单一镜面 symmetry 联系时可出现此现象。通过双格点双轨道模型演示：圆偏振光在有限交错势下诱导混合宇称交错磁性，零交错势下诱导奇宇称自旋-轨道交错磁性。自旋分辨轨道Edelstein效应为探测底层自旋-轨道序提供了补充电学手段。

item.title: 日冕磁零点附近快磁声波的非线性陡化
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yu Zhong, Valery M. Nakariakov, Mariana Cécere, Andrea Costa
该工作研究快磁声波与磁零点的相互作用及其在共生耀斑现象中的意义，关注非均匀等离子体环境中有限振幅效应导致的波陡化。采用无引导场的势磁场构型，从阿尔芬速度与声速相等距离外的脉冲点源激发快波。波沿磁场构型角平分线（垂直于局域磁场）向零点传播，因快波速度向零点降低而折射并放大非线性形变，可能在到达零点前即发生非线性耗散。

item.title: Provable imitation learning for control of instability in partially-observed Vlasov--Poisson equations
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Xiaofan Xia, Qin Li, Wenlong Mou
arXiv:2605.05081v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We consider the stabilization of Vlasov--Poisson plasma dynamics, a central control problem in nuclear fusion. Our focus is the gap between what an ideal controller would use and what experiments can actually observe: while optimal policy may rely on the full phase-space state, practical feedback is typically limited to sparse macroscopic diagnostics. We therefore study imitation learning methods that distill a fully observed expert policy into controllers operating only on macroscopic measurements. We show the stability guarantees of the learned policy, where the error floor depends on the minimal behavior cloning loss achievable under the observation constraints. We further characterize this minimal loss in terms of a notion of entropy t…

item.title: 量子相干重塑热机的热力学界限
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Sergi Vidal, Alba Mayor-Fernandez, Rosa Lopez
该工作以双端量子导体为模型，分析了热力学不确定关系（TUR）及其多维推广（MTUR）在热机、制冷机和热泵工作模式下的表现。研究表明，即使在相干输运主导区域，有限功率或冷热库间热流条件下的经典效率与性能系数极限仍受TUR约束；同时识别了TUR和MTUR违反的优化条件，证明近线性响应区交叉关联可增强电荷流与热流的联合精度。

item.title: Quantum Process Tomography of a Thermal Alkali-Metal Vapor
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yujie Sun, Marek Kopciuch, Arash Dezhang Fard, Szymon Pustelny
arXiv:2508.19634v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Characterizing the open-system dynamics of multilevel quantum systems (qudits) remains a fundamental challenge due to ensemble inhomogeneities and complex environmental interactions. Here, we introduce a computationally efficient quantum process tomography framework that reconstructs the Liouvillian dynamics of a thermal ⁸⁷Rb qutrit ensemble directly in the Bloch-Fano representation. By combining maximum likelihood estimation with post-hoc spectral regularization, our protocol extracts physically admissible, completely positive and trace-preserving maps without repeated numerical integration of the master equation. We rigorously justify selecting the principal branch for the matrix logarithm by demonstrating that experimental eigen…

item.title: 超导（约瑟夫森）二极管效应的响应张量
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Qiong Qin, Jie Wu, Congjun Wu
该工作提出响应张量ĥᵢ来表征超导（约瑟夫森）二极管效应的非互易临界电流响应，描述临界电流角分布偶极分量与外磁场的耦合，类比于正常态霍尔响应。在具有Rashba自旋轨道耦合及C₃ᵥ、C₄ᵥ或C₆ᵥ点群对称性的准二维系统中，该张量呈完全反对称形式；向列相出现时产生对称贡献，可作为超导态向列序的探针。对于Dresselhaus自旋轨道耦合的D₂d对称系统，张量为无迹对角形式，向列性引入迹项。该张量为探测向列性及潜在向列涨落提供新工具。

item.title: 基于量子与经典磁强计及惯性传感器的标量/矢量航空平台校准
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Antonia Hager, Torleiv H. Bryne, Mia Jukić
该工作重新评估航空磁测标准硬件配置，探讨钻石氮空位（NV）矢量磁强计实现全矢量校准的可行性。理论分析表明标量校准模型对失准具有鲁棒性，而矢量校准模型无论磁场测量精度如何，均对姿态误差具有一阶敏感性——源于体坐标系中背景场方向的不准确表征，可将微小取向误差放大为显著校准残差。该工作为地球物理勘探与磁异常导航的磁补偿策略提供理论依据。

item.title: 铁电光子器件中的散射诱导损耗
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jonah Townsend, Enzo Conceição Picinini, Rogério de Sousa
该工作建立微扰理论，将非吸收性（弹性）光子散射损耗表示为介电常数空间涨落一般谱密度的泛函。应用于平板波导衰减系数计算，比较了界面粗糙度与铁电畴无序两种损耗机制。理论可处理无特殊对称性要求的真实粗糙度，并展示如何利用铁电畴的电子显微图像获得衰减系数的显式数值预测。当平均畴长与光波长可比时（Mie区）损耗最大，表明对电信波长而言亚微米畴（Rayleigh区）或单畴波导是等效的降损策略。

item.title: 通过二维磁子晶体曲率雕刻自旋波景观
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Ondřej Wojewoda, Robert Kraft, Olha Bezsmertna, Oleksandr Pylypovskyi, Jose A. Fernandez Roldan, Caroline A. Ross, Rui Xu, Sergey A. Bunyaev, Ivan Soldatov, Rudolf Schäfer, Claas Abert, Gleb N. Kakazei, Michal Urbánek, Denys Makarov
该工作提出利用大面积曲线纳米模板（三维纳米金字塔方阵，周期400 nm）调控退磁场景观以操纵自旋波色散。在50 nm厚坡莫合金薄膜中，实验观测到完整面内带隙及金字塔谷处强实空间局域化的平带模。微聚焦布里渊光散射证实数值预测的色散关系，并揭示通过改变外磁场可开合带隙。该三维曲线方法避免传统二维磁子晶体刻蚀导致的衰减长度急剧下降，为自旋波能带工程开辟新途径。

item.title: YbB₁₂绝缘相中磁声量子振荡的探寻
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Ryosuke Kurihara, Atsuhiko Miyata, Koji Araki, Shusaku Imajo, Ruo Hibino, Atsushi Miyake, Sergei Zherlitsyn, Joachim Wosnitza, Hiroshi Yaguchi, Fumitoshi Iga, Masashi Tokunaga, Yasuhiro H. Matsuda
作者利用高达65 T强磁场和485 mK低温下的超声实验，对Kondo绝缘体YbB₁₂的体相量子振荡进行探寻。虽确认了磁电阻振荡及磁热效应中的场依赖振荡，但在绝缘态未观测到磁声量子振荡，仅在场诱导金属态发现此类信号。绝缘态中超声数据存在若干异常，其起源尚不明确，为理解该材料绝缘态的奇异行为提供了新线索。

item.title: 任意子的二次量子化与自旋-任意子对偶
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Priyanshi Bhasin, Diptiman Sen, Tanmoy Das
该工作为统计相位θ=π/N的一维阿贝尔任意子建立了代数框架，实现有限在位占据数N-1与不同格点间交换相位的自洽表述，并引入π/3任意子与自旋-1算符的精确Jordan-Wigner对偶。将紧束缚任意子模型映射为XY型自旋-1模型，发现任意子密度依赖的磁通、不可压缩/无能隙区域及基态电流、动量、保真度和关联函数中的不连续临界行为，为从自旋哈密顿量实现任意子提供了新途径。

item.title: YIG薄膜/CoFeB纳米盘磁子晶体中通过模式杂化实现自旋波带隙工程
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Junyoung Hyun, Krzysztof Szulc, Mateusz Zelent, Nikolai Kuznetsov, Lukáš Flajšman, Maciej Krawczyk, Paweł Gruszecki, Sebastiaan van Dijken
该工作研究低阻尼钇铁石榴石(YIG)薄膜与CoFeB纳米盘周期阵列构成的二维混合磁子晶体中的自旋波输运。实验结合传播自旋波谱学、超奈奎斯特磁光克尔效应显微术与微磁模拟，证实由磁子晶体基模与纳米盘诱导的面内横向驻模杂化形成显著可调带隙，其机制超越传统布拉格散射。带隙的位置与宽度受几何参数及纳米盘磁态（包括涡旋构型）调控，大晶格周期时出现二维色散折叠导致的额外杂化带隙。

item.title: 三维球极化磁场
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Anna Tenerani, Marco Velli
该工作提出一种精确构造球极化磁场的新数值方法，使磁场强度|B|严格保持恒定，重现太阳风湍流的关键特征。研究表明三维光滑磁场无法在大尺度空间域内同时满足恒定|B|约束，场旋转必然伴随间断面出现；恒定幅值的场旋转仅存在于有限空间区域，其间以不可忽略磁压缩性的间断面分隔。该结果为理解太阳风中的开关事件（switchbacks）等磁场极性反转现象提供了理论基础。

item.title: 纠缠磁场中的电子输运
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Daniel Verscharen, Natasha Jeffrey, Anton Artemyev, Jesse T. Coburn, Matthew W. Kunz, Oreste Pezzi, Mario Riquelme, Ida Svenningsson, Lynn B. Wilson III
该综述介绍空间等离子体中电子在由湍流和不稳定性产生的非均匀、高度纠缠磁场中的输运基本原理。当磁场变化尺度远大于电子回旋半径时，电子主要沿磁力线做导向中心运动；导向中心漂移、波粒相互作用、捕获效应及跨场扩散等过程可改变甚至破坏沿场输运。文章系统回顾了磁场纠缠的成因、动力学不稳定性对平行电子输运的调制、非均匀场中的捕获-去捕获效应，以及电子跨场扩散与能量化机制。

item.title: 亚3 nm镧系掺杂双钙钛矿量子点：热增强上转换纳米测温与磁共振成像的多功能平台
item.description:
📚 ACS Nano: Latest Articles (ACS Publications)
👤 Ruitong Song, Sen Yan, Shunju Liu, Yu Zhang, Qiuhan Liu, Jiayao Li, Heping Deng, Peng Zhou, Yue Zhao, and Hao Fu
该工作围绕亚3 nm镧系掺杂双钙钛矿量子点的合成与多功能应用展开，发展了热增强上转换纳米测温与磁共振成像的一体化平台。具体性能参数与机制细节需查阅原文。

item.title: Anion correlation induced nonrelativistic spin splitting in rutile antiferromagnets
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Materials
👤 Siddhartha S. Nathan, Danilo Puggioni, Linding Yuan, and James M. Rondinelli
Author(s): Siddhartha S. Nathan, Danilo Puggioni, Linding Yuan, and James M. Rondinelli The search for altermagnetic and related materials has largely focused on ideal crystals, overlooking the role of short-range chemical order. Using density functional theory and a cluster expansion model, the authors show that short-range anion correlations in iron oxyfluoride (FeOF) tune the magnitude and character of nonrelativistic spin splitting, producing splittings absent in ordered FeF 2 and virtual-crystal approximation models. The authors further identify magneto-optical Kerr spectroscopy as a route to detect these effects experimentally and highlight heteroanionic substitution as a design strategy for high- T N spin-split antiferromagnets. [Phys. Rev. Materials 10, 054404] Published Thu May 0…

item.title: 玻璃态网络材料中的多非晶相现象
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Max Hall-Brown, Peter Guy Wolynes
该工作构建微观模型研究网络液体在低温高压下多非晶相（polyamorphism）的玻璃态与热力学性质，探讨成键与非键局域构型竞争导致的液-液相变及其与玻璃转变温度的相对位置关系。模型通过调节微观参数可使相变位于玻璃转变之上、附近或之下，为理解水等材料中密度异常等现象提供简化理论框架。

item.title: Reservoir Computing‐Based Glucose Sensing With an Enzymatic Reaction Network
item.description:
📚 Wiley
👤 Souvik Ghosh, Anna C. Knox, Bin Li, Wilhelm T. S. Huck
Angewandte Chemie International Edition, EarlyView.

item.title: 训练时批归一化重塑分段仿射网络的局部分区几何
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Xuan Qi, Yi Wei, Fanqi Yu, Furao shen, Vittorio Murino, Cigdem Beyan
该工作从函数几何层面研究训练时批归一化（BN）对连续分段仿射（CPA）网络的影响。证明BN为每个神经元定义通过批次质心的参考超平面，且断点切换超平面为平行平移，其偏移量以批次标准化坐标表达并与原始偏置无关。在显式充分条件下，BN增加ReLU及更一般分段仿射网络的期望局部分区细化，且该机制在父仿射区域内通过深度局部传递。结果为BN的函数级几何效应提供了严格理论刻画。
